subject: Accounting
title: Financial Statement Audits
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Financial Statement Audits.

hook.text: The most common audit: are the financial statements free from material misstatement?

buildup.text: Financial statement audits examine the balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement.

discovery.text: Public companies must have annual audits by law. Private companies often choose to for credibility.

twist.text: The audit doesn't test every number—it focuses on areas with the highest risk of error or fraud.

climax.text: A clean opinion lets companies raise capital, attract investors, and maintain market confidence.

punchline.text: The financial audit is the gold standard of trust in numbers.

quiz.question: Which companies are required to have financial audits?
